name: disambiguate_work_titles_by_type_and_year
patch: If the question names a work with an ambiguous title, emit multiple short BM25-style queries targeting each plausible work-type and year: e.g. 'TITLE (film) cast', 'TITLE (YEAR film) cast', 'TITLE (miniseries) cast', 'TITLE (TV series) cast'. Keep each query compact and keyword-like; separate queries with semicolons.
gradient_intent: Move queries toward retrieving specific work pages (film/miniseries/TV) by adding type+year disambiguators so cast/credits passages are found.
preservation_intent: Do not add long natural-language phrasing or names; keep queries short and avoid adding extra unrelated keywords so already-correct short queries still match BM25 behavior.

name: expand_cast_and_credit_keywords_for_shared_cast
patch: When the question asks whether an actor/actress 'starred in', 'appeared in', or 'shared cast' across works, append compact variants using cast/credit synonyms: 'TITLE cast', 'TITLE starring', 'TITLE cast list', 'TITLE filmography credited', and also a combined query like 'TITLE1 TITLE2 shared cast appeared in both'. Separate by semicolons.
gradient_intent: Encourage retrieval of passages that list cast members or filmography entries (including ensemble casts) so shared-cast links are found.
preservation_intent: Avoid inserting candidate actor names or long descriptions; keep keyword-only queries so existing correct queries are unaffected.

name: national_team_and_home_stadium_patterns
patch: For questions about players and 'national team' or 'home matches', generate compact queries that search both player→team and team→stadium relations: e.g. 'PLAYER national team', 'PLAYER played for', 'PLAYER (country) footballer', plus 'COUNTRY national football team home stadium', 'plays most of its home matches at', 'hosts most of its home matches'. Use short keyword phrases; do not expand into full sentences.
gradient_intent: Push queries to retrieve the player's national team page and the national team's stadium/home-match wording so the stadium answer appears.
preservation_intent: Keep queries focused and concise; do not add broad geographic terms that would harm precision on already-correct examples.

name: ordinal_album_and_discography_release_query_variants
patch: When the question asks for an album 'nth' (e.g., 'eleventh') or asks about a band's numbered studio album, produce compact discography-focused queries: 'BAND discography', 'BAND "nth studio album"', 'BAND "studio album #N" release date', 'BAND "album name" release date' (if an album title emerges from first-hop). Also include short queries combining superlatives if the question describes the band (e.g., 'highest-selling Canadian heavy metal' + 'discography').
gradient_intent: Encourage retrieval of pages that list album order and release dates (discography / album pages) so the correct release date is obtainable.
preservation_intent: Do not hardcode album titles or dates; keep queries compact and avoid adding many unrelated music keywords to preserve BM25 precision on correct examples.

name: add_alt_names_and_hashtags
patch: When the question asks for an "alternate/other/also called/another name" or mentions an online campaign, append compact alternate-name variants to the query: include quoted full-name variants and short nickname forms, add common "aka" forms and hashtag variants (remove spaces for hashtags, e.g. #occupyflagstaffhouse), and join these variants with OR. Keep each variant as keyword-like tokens, do not add explanatory text or full sentences.
gradient_intent: push queries toward including alternate names, nickname forms and hashtag variants so BM25 can match pages that use the campaign/alias (fixes misses like Occupy Flagstaff House).
preservation_intent: avoid turning queries into sentences; maintain compact keyword-like variants and do not inject gold answers or long prose.

name: expand_place_name_variants_and_country_tokens
patch: For place/county/location questions, add terse location-name variants: include 'County X', 'Co. X', 'X County', and common alternative names (single-word short forms) joined by OR. Also append concise country-location tokens like 'which country', 'located in', 'part of' and common country-name variants (contemporary AND common historical terms) as keywords — but do not pick or assert an answer.
gradient_intent: encourage queries to cover alternate place spellings/abbreviations and both modern/historical country tokens so BM25 finds pages that use any of those forms (fixes misses for County Londonderry/Northern Ireland).
preservation_intent: keep queries compact and non-assertive (no full sentences or answers); do not bias toward one country name over others.

name: person_name_variants_and_event_keywords
patch: When the question targets a person or a disciplinary/event detail, append short name variants (full name, last name, initial+last) joined by OR, and add concise event/action keywords relevant to the predicate (e.g. suspend* OR suspension OR violation OR PED OR 'domestic violence') and explicit year tokens or year-range tokens if dates are in scope. Keep everything as isolated keywords or quoted phrases; avoid narrative text.
gradient_intent: move queries to include alternate person-name tokens plus relevant event verbs and years so BM25 returns pages mentioning the person in the context asked (helps locate suspension info like 2013/2014).
preservation_intent: do not append long explanations or extra non-keyword text; avoid adding excessive irrelevant verbs that would change retrieval class on already-correct person/name queries.

name: prefer_or_keyword_compactness_and_stopword_pruning
patch: Favor compact, BM25-friendly queries: prune stopwords, keep only informative lexemes, and express alternatives with OR instead of natural-language connectors. Only add expansions that introduce distinct search tokens (names, years, locations, hashtags). If adding multiple variants, limit to the top ~6 variants to avoid noise. Do not convert the query into a full question sentence.
gradient_intent: encourage denser, keyword-rich queries with controlled OR expansions so BM25 weighting favors relevant documents while avoiding dilution by stopwords or long phrasing.
preservation_intent: preserve current correct behavior for queries that are already effective (do not add unnecessary variants or length) and maintain short keyword-like form.

name: alias_and_variant_expansion
patch: When generating the hop2 query, automatically expand core entity names into short, keyword-style variants and aliases joined by OR. Include likely alternate names, common abbreviations, and parenthetical disambiguators (e.g. "(TV series)") as quoted multiword tokens when appropriate (keep each alternative compact). Prefer OR-connected tokens over long natural-language clauses.
gradient_intent: move queries to cover alternate names/aliases and disambiguators so BM25 can match pages that use different surface forms
preservation_intent: do not add verbose sentences or unrelated synonyms; keep queries short and only add alternatives when they are tightly relevant to the target entity

name: list_pages_and_structured_episode_tokens
patch: For media/episode questions, append compact structured tokens and list-page signals: include 'List of <Series> episodes', 'season N', 'episode N', 'S1E4' (and common SxEx variants), 'episode title', and the series name with '(TV series)' disambiguator. Combine these with OR and keep tokens terse.
gradient_intent: bias queries toward episode-list or episode-article pages by adding list-style and season/episode tokens that BM25 prioritizes
preservation_intent: only apply these structured tokens when the question context implies a media/episode lookup; do not add for unrelated question types

name: location_org_qualifiers
patch: When the question involves organizations, facilities, or where-situated entities, add compact location and organizational qualifiers: keywords like 'based in', 'headquartered', 'located in', plus candidate place names (city/state/country) and facility types (hospital/clinic/foundation). Combine candidate org names with OR and keep queries keyword-like rather than full sentences.
gradient_intent: nudge queries to retrieve pages that state organizational headquarters or facility locations by including location/organization phrases and place-name tokens
preservation_intent: avoid adding extraneous place tokens that can introduce noise; only include place qualifiers suggested by the question context

name: online_movement_and_hashtag_forms
patch: For protests/online-movement questions, include compact social-form variants: the movement name, hashtag forms (with and without #, concatenated lowercase), common nickname forms (no spaces), and the phrase 'online protest' or 'hashtag'. Join these alternatives with OR and quote multiword tokens; keep the overall query concise.
gradient_intent: encourage retrieval of pages that use hashtag/online labels or alternate concatenated names by adding hashtag and nickname variants
preservation_intent: do not insert hashtags or social-media tokens for questions that are not about online movements or campaigns
